subject: Re: using WARP to extract subvolumes from AREtomo tomograms
body:
Hi Jesse,
You cannot "trick" Warp into extracting subtomos from tomograms. If
you want to follow that approach you are better off doing this in
another software package (IMOD/PEET is not a bad place to start). The
tomograms reconstructed by Warp are strictly for visualisation and
particle picking purposes. The actual subtomos are reconstructed from
the averaged frames with CTF-premultiplication. These CTF corrected
subtomos appear noisy to our eyes, but they are perfectly visible to
the refinement engines. However, if you do want to "boost" the
contrast to a. Remove junk picks, b. get starting angles, then you
could bin subvolumes heavily (bin4, bin5? depends on the size of the
object you are trying to visualise, and the pixel size, you can bin
indiscriminately as you need enough pixels to distinguis your molecule
from the background noise).

hope this helps.
Best,
Pranav
--
Pranav Shah
Postdoctoral Research Fellow.

Division of Structural Biology,
Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
University of Oxford,
Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
UK

On Fri, Dec 1, 2023 at 10:32 AM Jesse Hansen <bluufive@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> hi all,
>
> I know this must sound ridiculous, but I'm wondering whether it's possible to somehow provide WARP with an AREtomo reconstruction then do subtomo extraction on it.  I guess not, since WARP does "on-the-fly" subtomo generation using either frames or averaged/aligned movies.   This is fundamentally different than packages which simply crop the tomogram to generate subvolumes (e.g. dynamo?). Since my understanding is that AREtomo will produce better subtomos than WARP early on, I could use those subvolumes to obtain orientations then later switch back to the WARP/RELION/M pipeline.
>
> Second question: is the on-the-fly processing done from the frames or from the aligned/averaged movies.  I ask because if it's the latter can I conceivably alter contrast of some of those averages and have that take affect in the tomo/subtomo recon?
>
> thanks!
>
> Jesse
>
> --
